label: Splunk
description: Send and query log events and security analytics data from Splunk via HEC or the REST API.

form.title: Connect Splunk
form.subtitle: Ingest events or query Splunk data by connecting Anton to your Splunk instance.

methods.label: HTTP Event Collector (HEC) Token
methods.description: Paste your Splunk instance URL and an HEC token to send log events directly to Splunk. The simplest path for data ingestion — no username or password required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via HTTP Event Collector (HEC) Token

HEC lets Anton push events to Splunk over HTTP using a token-based auth. No username or password is stored.

### Steps

1. Log in to your Splunk instance (Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud).
2. Navigate to **Settings → Data Inputs → HTTP Event Collector**.
3. Click **New Token** in the top-right corner.
4. Give the token a name like `Anton` and click through the wizard, selecting an appropriate source type and index.
5. On the final screen, copy the **Token Value** shown (it looks like a UUID). This is shown once — save it.
6. If HEC is not yet enabled, click **Global Settings** on the HEC page and toggle **All Tokens** to **Enabled**. Note the port (default `8088`).
7. Paste your Splunk instance URL (e.g. `https://your-splunk-host:8088`) and the token into the fields below.

### Notes

- For Splunk Cloud, the HEC endpoint is typically `https://<stack>.splunkcloud.com:8088`.
- Ensure your network/firewall allows outbound connections to port 8088.
- If you lose the token, create a new one under **Settings → Data Inputs → HTTP Event Collector**.

fields.name: instance_url
fields.label: Splunk Instance URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Splunk instance including the HEC port (default 8088). Example: https://splunk.company.com:8088

fields.name: hec_token
fields.label: HEC Token
fields.description: The token value generated from Settings → Data Inputs → HTTP Event Collector. Shown once at creation time.

fields.name: index
fields.label: Default Index
fields.description: Optional. Splunk index to send events to. Defaults to the index configured on the HEC token if left blank.

fields.name: ssl_verify
fields.label: Verify SSL Certificate
fields.description: Disable only if your Splunk instance uses a self-signed certificate. Strongly recommended to leave enabled in production.

methods.label: Splunk Auth Token (REST API)
methods.description: Use a Splunk authentication token from Settings → Tokens to query search jobs and other REST API endpoints. Best for reading and searching data from Splunk.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Splunk Auth Token (REST API)

Splunk supports long-lived authentication tokens for programmatic REST API access. This method lets Anton run searches and retrieve results from your Splunk instance.

### Steps

1. Log in to Splunk Web.
2. Go to **Settings → Users and Authentication → Tokens**.
3. Click **New Token**.
4. Set an **Audience** (e.g. `Anton`) and optionally an expiry date. A non-expiring token is simpler but review your security policy.
5. Click **Create** and copy the token string immediately — it is only shown once.
6. Note your Splunk REST API port (default `8089`) and paste the full instance URL (e.g. `https://your-splunk-host:8089`) and token below.

### Notes

- Token-based auth requires Splunk Enterprise 7.3+ or Splunk Cloud.
- The REST API port is typically `8089`, separate from the HEC port `8088`.
- If the **Tokens** menu is missing, token authentication may be disabled by your admin. Ask them to enable it under **Settings → Users and Authentication → Tokens → Token Settings**.
- Tokens can be revoked at any time from the same **Tokens** page.

fields.name: instance_url
fields.label: Splunk Instance URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Splunk REST API endpoint including the management port (default 8089). Example: https://splunk.company.com:8089

fields.name: auth_token
fields.label: Auth Token
fields.description: The token string from Settings → Users and Authentication → Tokens. Shown only once at creation time.

fields.name: default_app
fields.label: Default App Context
fields.description: Optional. Splunk app context for search jobs (e.g. search, itsi). Defaults to 'search' if left blank.

fields.name: ssl_verify
fields.label: Verify SSL Certificate
fields.description: Disable only if your Splunk instance uses a self-signed certificate. Strongly recommended to leave enabled in production.
